patterns.description: Consistent 'permission string as local constant in route' pattern: route files define local string constants for permissions (e.g., SELLER_ADMINS_READ, SELLER_ADMINS_DELETE). This is repeated across multiple admin route files. Consider creating a shared permissions constants module at apps/admin/src/shared/domain/permissions.ts to centralize all permission strings used in route authorization.
patterns.suggestion: Create apps/admin/src/shared/domain/permissions.ts exporting SELLER_ADMINS_READ, SELLER_ADMINS_DELETE, etc. This prevents the current pattern where each route redefines the same strings.

patterns.description: Pattern: Context + hook co-location is consistently used across CartContext.tsx, FlyToCartContext.tsx, ErrorContext.tsx. This is a recognized React pattern. Recommend documenting this as an approved exception to the one-component-per-file rule in the component-patterns rule file.
patterns.suggestion: Add to component-patterns.md: 'Context files may export both the Provider component and its hook (e.g., CartProvider + useCart). This is an approved Context pattern exception.'

patterns.description: The paymentMethodIdRef pattern (updating a ref to current value before callbacks to prevent stale closures) is used in useAutoSavePaymentMethod.ts. This is a well-known React pattern. If more hooks need this pattern, a custom useLatestRef(value) hook could be added to packages/shared.
patterns.suggestion: Consider adding useLatestRef<T>(value: T): React.RefObject<T> to packages/shared/src/hooks/ for reuse.

patterns.description: The 'eslint-disable react-hooks/exhaustive-deps -- intentional: only trigger on [specific] changes' pattern is used in useAutoSavePaymentMethod.ts for intentional dependency array omissions. This is well-documented but should be monitored - overuse can hide real bugs.
patterns.suggestion: Keep tracking intentional exhaustive-deps suppressions. Current usage in useAutoSavePaymentMethod.ts is justified and well-commented.
